title: C. elegans Extraction Protocol for LC-MS Analysis
author: Brianna M Garcia
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bahjib4n
materials: Fisher Scientific Isotemp High Speed Shaker

steps.instruction: (3) 2.0mm zirconium oxide beads and ~ 75 µL volume of 0.5mm glass beads are added to each sample tube.

steps.title: Samples are placed in Tissuelyser II...
steps.instruction: Samples are placed in Tissuelyser II using adapter trays chilled at -80C and homogenized at 1800rpm for 00:03:00 .

steps.title: 750 µL of 100% isopropanol is added to the 1
steps.instruction: 750 µL of 100% isopropanol is added to the 1.5mL Eppendorf tube containing the homogenized sample. The sample tube is lightly vortexed to create a suspension of homogenized sample and the resulting slurry is transferred to a new 2.0mL Eppendorf tube, leaving the beads behind in the original tube. This step is repeated so that a total of 1.5 mL of solvent is transferred to the new 2.0mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Samples are placed in the centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Samples are placed in the centrifuge and spun at max speed (22100G) for 00:05:00

steps.title: Supernatant of each sample is transferred to a new 2
steps.instruction: Supernatant of each sample is transferred to a new 2.0 mL Eppendorf labeled for RP chromatography and dried down using steps 14 through 18

steps.title: The second round of the sequential extraction is 1
steps.instruction: The second round of the sequential extraction is 1.5 mL of 80/20 methanol/water per added directly to the pellet remaining after centrifugation.

steps.title: Samples are shaken using the Fisher...
steps.instruction: Samples are shaken using the Fisher Scientific Isotemp High Speed Shaker at 1500rpm for 00:30:00
2.0mL Eppendorfs are placed in the centrifuge and spun at max speed (22100G) for 00:05:00

steps.title: Supernatant of each sample is transferred to a new 2
steps.instruction: Supernatant of each sample is transferred to a new 2.0 mL Eppendorf labeled for HILIC chromatography and dried down using steps 14 through 18

steps.title: Samples are placed in a Labconco...
steps.instruction: Samples are placed in a Labconco CentriVap concentrator and monitored until they have completely dried (roughly 4-5 hours)

steps.title: Once dry, samples are stored at...
steps.instruction: Once dry, samples are stored at -80 °C until they are to be run on the LC-MS instrument.

steps.title: When preparing samples to run on...
steps.instruction: When preparing samples to run on LC-MS, reconstitute samples with 75 µL of 100% isopropanol for reverse phase and 80/20 methanol/water for HILIC.

steps.title: After LC-MS analysis, samples can be...
steps.instruction: After LC-MS analysis, samples can be stored at -80C until ready for IMS analysis

steps.title: Samples are vortexed for 00:01:00 and...
steps.instruction: Samples are vortexed for 00:01:00 and then extracted over night at -20C

steps.title: Pellets are dried for 1 hour...
steps.instruction: Pellets are dried for 1 hour and stored at -80 °C

steps.title: Vortex for 1 minute, then centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Vortex for 1 minute, then centrifuge at max speed (22100G) for 00:05:00
